ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan – Summary
1. What is ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan? ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan is a non-linked non-participating individual pension plan, that helps you accumulate a guaranteed corpus for your retirement along with a life insurance cover. This lump sum corpus allows you to secure an uninterrupted income throughout your golden years.
2. What are the significant features of ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan? Key features of ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan are:
• Fully guaranteed corpus accumulated on your retirement helping you secure a regular income for your golden years. • Guaranteed Additions during the Policy Term to build your retirement corpus.
• Loyalty Addition added at the end of the Policy Term to further boost your retirement corpus, provided all due premiums have been paid.
• Build your own plan by choosing between varied Premium Payment Terms – invest for the

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP) • Build your own plan by choosing between varied Premium Payment Terms – invest for the entire policy term or for a limited period to suit your requirements.• Life Insurance Cover to offer financial protection for your loved ones, equal to total premiums paid accumulated at a fixed percentage during the Policy Term. • Flexibility to defer your Vesting Benefit by up to 10 years to ensure your retirement planning always stays aligned with your life goals.3. What are the age criteria for this Plan?A simple entry criterion is developed for enabling ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan, the minimum and maximum entry ages applicable for the Life Insured are as follows: • Minimum Age at entry: 30 years • Maximum Age at entry: 65 years4. What are the minimum and maximum vesting ages applicable for this plan? ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan offers a minimum vesting age of 45 years and a maximum vesting age of 75 years.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP) ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan offers a minimum vesting age of 45 years and a maximum vesting age of 75 years.Plan Structure At inception policyholder chooses – • Premium Payment Term • Policy Term • Premium Amount • Premium Frequency 5. What is the Premium Payment Term (PPT) under this plan? ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan offers Limited and Regular premium payment terms. Under limited premium payment term, you can choose to pay premiums for 5, 6, 8 or 10 years. Under regular premium payment term, you have to pay premiums for a period equal to Policy Term, that ranges from 10 years to 25 years. However, currently 12 Pay and Regular Pay are not available for sale. 6. What is the Policy Term under this plan? ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan offers a Policy Term for a period of 10 years to 25 years. 9. What are the different modes of payment available and is there any loading applicable on any mode? You can pay premiums in different modes as per your requirement.

Benefits 10. What is the Vesting Benefit available in the plan? On survival of the Life Insured till the end of the Policy Term, your Vesting Benefit will be the sum of the following: • Guaranteed Vesting Benefit; plus • Accrued Guaranteed Additions till the end of the Policy Term; plus • Loyalty Addition, if any. Where, ‘Guaranteed Vesting Benefit’ is the amount guaranteed to become payable on the date of vesting, in accordance with the terms and conditions of the policy. This is expressed as a percentage of Total Premiums Payable. ‘Guaranteed Additions’ and ‘Loyalty Addition’ are explained in detail in question no.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)
 This is expressed as a percentage of Total Premiums Payable.
‘Guaranteed Additions’ and ‘Loyalty Addition’ are explained in detail in question no. 12 below, please refer the same.
11. How can I utilize the Vesting Benefit?
You may choose from the below 3 options, regulation mandates the Policyholder to utilize the proceeds of the Vesting Benefit as below:
1. Utilize the entire proceeds to purchase immediate annuity or deferred annuity from us at the then prevailing annuity rate or subject to the point 3 below, you shall be given an option to purchase immediate annuity or deferred annuity from any other insurer; or
2. Commute up to 60% and utilize the balance amount to purchase immediate annuity or deferred annuity from us at the then prevailing annuity rate or subject to the point 3 below, you shall be given an option to purchase available annuity from any other insurer.
3. Policyholder shall be given an option to purchase immediate annuity or deferred annuity from another insurer at the then prevailing annuity rate to the extent of percentage, stipulated by the Authority, currently 50%, of the entire proceeds of the policy net

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)
annuity or deferred annuity from another insurer at the then prevailing annuity rate to the extent of percentage, stipulated by the Authority, currently 50%, of the entire proceeds of the policy net of commutation.
The purchase of annuity shall be subject to terms and conditions under the annuity product.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP) Guaranteed Additions are defined as a fixed percentage of Total Premiums Payable which get accrued into your policy as per the premium payment term chosen: • For a Limited Pay policy, Guaranteed Additions accrue at the end of each policy year starting from the end of the policy year following the Premium Payment Term till the date of vesting i.e., till the end of the Policy Term. • For a Regular Pay policy, Guaranteed Additions accrue at the end of each policy year starting from the end of the first policy year onwards till the date of vesting i.e., till the end of the Policy Term. Accrued Guaranteed Additions shall be paid on the date of vesting.

13. Can Policyholder defer the vesting benefit? Yes, at any time during the Policy Term, provided the policy is in-force and all due premiums are paid, if Policyholder does not wish to receive the Vesting Benefit at the chosen Vesting Age, he will have the option to defer this benefit by a Deferral Period of 1 year to 10 years from the date of vesting. 14. Will there be life cover during the deferment period of Vesting Benefit? No, there will not be any life cover during deferment period.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)
 Will there be life cover during the deferment period of Vesting Benefit? No, there will not be any life cover during deferment period.
15. Can the policyholder take a commuted value of vesting benefits during the Policy Term? No, commutation benefit is not offered under this plan.
16. What is the Death Benefit payable under this plan?
In the event of death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term and provided that the Policy is In-force, the Death Benefit will be payable to the Nominee(s)/Legal heir(s). Nomination can only be affected if the Policyholder and Life Insured are the same.
We will pay a lump sum “Death Benefit” which shall be the higher of:
• Sum Assured on Death
• Surrender Value acquired by the policy till the date of death
Where, The Sum Assured on Death is defined as the higher of:
• Total Premiums Paid to date accumulated at a guaranteed rate of 6% p.a. compounded annually.
• 105% of the Total Premiums Paid to date.
EXAMPLE

17. Can the Death Benefit be taken in instalments?
No, this plan does not offer Death Benefit to be taken in instalments.
18. Is the Death Benefit payable during the policy term?
Yes, Death Benefit is payable in case of death of the Life Insured during of the Policy Term.
19. When does the Policy acquire a Surrender Value?
Policy acquires a Surrender Value once premiums for at least two full policy years have been paid.
20. What is the Surrender Benefit under this plan?
The surrender value payable to policyholder will be higher of
• Guaranteed Surrender Value or
• Special Surrender Value
The policy shall be terminated once the Surrender Value is paid.
Policy shall also be eligible for a Special Surrender Value; this Special Surrender Value is not guaranteed and can be reviewed by ABSLI from time to time.
On surrendering the policy, the policyholder can:
1. Utilize the entire proceeds to purchase immediate annuity or deferred annuity from ABSLI at the then prevailing annuity rate or

or
2. Commute up to 60% and utilize the balance amount to purchase immediate annuity or deferred annuity from ABSLI at the then prevailing annuity rate or subject to the point 3 below, the policyholder shall be given an option to purchase available annuity from any other insurer.
3. Every policyholder shall be given an option to purchase immediate annuity or deferred annuity from another insurer at the then prevailing annuity rate to the extent of percentage, stipulated by the Authority, currently 50%, of the entire proceeds of the policy net of commutation.

In case the proceeds of the policy on surrender is not sufficient to purchase minimum annuity as defined in Regulation as amended from time to time, such proceeds of the policy shall be paid to the policyholder as lump sum.
21. What happens in case of death of the Life Insured by suicide?
In case the Life Insured, either minor or major, dies due to suicide within 12 months from the Risk Commencement Date or date of Revival of the Policy, the Policy shall immediately terminate, and Company shall pay the following to the Nominee:
• Where the Policy has acquired the Surrender Value, higher of Surrender Value or (Total Premiums Paid plus underwriting extra premiums paid plus loadings for modal premiums paid excluding applicable taxes) till date of death.
• Where the Policy hasn’t acquired the Surrender Value, Total Premiums Paid plus underwriting extra premiums paid plus loadings for modal premiums paid excluding applicable taxes) till date of death.
22. Is there any rider benefit in this plan?

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)

• Where the Policy hasn’t acquired the Surrender Value, Total Premiums Paid plus underwriting extra premiums paid plus loadings for modal premiums paid excluding applicable taxes) till date of death.
22. Is there any rider benefit in this plan? No, rider benefits are not available under this plan.
23. Is loan facility available under this plan?
No, loan is not available under the plan.
24. Is Top-up available under this plan?
No, top-up is not available under the plan.
Premium Discontinuance and Revival
25. What happens if policyholder does not pay premiums on time?
If policyholder is unable to pay premium by the due date, he / she will be given a grace period of 30 days (15 days for monthly mode policies) and during this grace period all coverage under this policy will continue.
26. What happens if policyholder does not pay premium within the grace period?
In case the premium is not paid by the expiry of the Grace Period, the following provisions will apply:
• Discontinuance of Payment of Premium before the policy has acquired Surrender Value
If you do not pay the due premium during the Grace Period, in the first 2 policy years i.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)
provisions will apply:
• Discontinuance of Payment of Premium before the policy has acquired Surrender Value
If you do not pay the due premium during the Grace Period, in the first 2 policy years i.e., when the policy has not acquired surrender value, on expiry of the Grace Period, the Policy shall Lapse w.e.f. the due date of unpaid premium, and all benefits under the policy, including the risk cover, shall cease and no benefits shall be payable. However, you will have the option to revive the Policy within 5 years from the due date of first unpaid premium.
• Discontinuance of Payment of Premium after the policy has acquired Surrender Value
If you don’t pay the due premium during the Grace Period, on expiry of the Grace Period, the policy shall be converted into a Reduced Paid-Up (RPU) Policy post which the following benefits shall be payable under the policy.
27. What are the terms and conditions applicable for revival of this policy?
Policyholder can revive the Policy within a revival period of five years from the due date of first unpaid premium, subject to following conditions:
• Paying all outstanding premiums together with interest and/or late fees as declared by us from

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)
a revival period of five years from the due date of first unpaid premium, subject to following conditions:
• Paying all outstanding premiums together with interest and/or late fees as declared by us from time to time.
• Providing evidence of Life Insured’s insurability satisfactory to us.
Revival of the Policy shall take effect only after revival of the Policy is approved by Us basis the Board approved underwriting policy and communicated to you in writing.
Once the Policy has been revived, on the effective date of revival, all benefits will be restored to their full value.
The monthly interest rate charged on unpaid premiums will be declared by ABSLI on June 1st of each calendar year and is determined as (x+1%)/12 rounded to the next 0.5%, where x is the base rate of the State Bank of India. The interest rate, as declared on June 1st, 2022, is 1% per month. (compounded).

Any change in basis of determination of interest rate for revival can be done only after prior approval of the IRDAI.
28. What happens if a policy is not revived within the revival period?
In case the policy is not revived within the revival period, the policy cannot be revived thereafter and the Lapsed Policy shall stand terminated with no benefits payable thereafter.
However, a Reduced Paid- Up policy shall continue under the Reduced Paid-up mode till the end of the policy term.
29. What is the Vesting Benefit for a Reduced Paid-up policy?
The RPU Guaranteed Vesting Benefit shall be equal to the Guaranteed Vesting Benefit respectively multiplied by the RPU Factor.
Where, “RPU Factor” means the ratio of the number of Instalment Premium paid on date, over the total number of Instalment Premium originally due for the Policy Term.
For a Reduced Paid-Up (RPU) Policy, accrued Guaranteed Additions, at the time of RPU, shall not be reduced and remain attached to the policy.
An RPU policy shall be entitled to the percentage of the future Guaranteed Additions, depending upon the number of full year premiums

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)

An RPU policy shall be entitled to the percentage of the future Guaranteed Additions, depending upon the number of full year premiums paid.
A Regular Pay RPU policy shall not be entitled to any future Guaranteed Additions
Your Policy will not be entitled for any Loyalty Addition once it has been converted into a Reduced Paid-Up Policy.
Vesting Benefit for RPU Policies
On your survival until the end of the Policy Term, the RPU Vesting Benefit shall be:
• RPU Guaranteed Vesting Benefit; plus
• Accrued Guaranteed Additions till the date the policy becomes an RPU policy
The proceeds of RPU Vesting Benefit will have to be utilized as per the provisions mentioned in the Vesting Benefit section.
30. What would be the death benefit for a Reduced Paid-up policy during the policy term? If the Life Insured dies during the Policy Term, RPU Death benefit shall be
Higher of:
• Sum Assured on Death
• Surrender Benefit as on the date of Death
The proceeds of the RPU Death Benefit will have to be utilized as per the provisions mentioned in the Question no.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)
shall be
Higher of:
• Sum Assured on Death
• Surrender Benefit as on the date of Death
The proceeds of the RPU Death Benefit will have to be utilized as per the provisions mentioned in the Question no. 15.
31. What would be the Survival benefit for a reduced paid-up policy?
The Survival benefit for a reduced paid-up policy will be sum of:
• RPU Guaranteed Vesting Benefit; plus
• Accrued Guaranteed Additions till the date the policy becomes an RPU policy
The proceeds of the RPU Vesting Benefit will have to be utilized as per the provisions mentioned in the Question no. 11.
32. What is the Free-look Period applicable for this policy?
You will have the right to return Your Policy to us within 15 days (30 days in case of electronic policies and the policies issued under the provisions of IRDAI Guidelines on Distance Marketing of Insurance products) from the date of receipt of the Policy, in case You are not satisfied with the terms & conditions of Your Policy.
33. In case I opt for Freelook, will my premium amount be refunded?
We will refund the premium paid once we receive Your written notice of cancellation (along with reasons thereof) together with the original Policy document.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)

We will refund the premium paid once we receive Your written notice of cancellation (along with reasons thereof) together with the original Policy document. We may reduce the amount of the refund by proportionate risk premium for the period of cover

and expenses incurred by us on medical examination and stamp duty charges while issuing Your Policy in accordance to IRDAI (Protection of Policyholders Interest) Regulations, 2017.
Termination
34. When will this policy terminate?
Policy will get terminated on any of the following events:
(a) The date of payment of Surrender Value under the policy; or
(b) The date of settlement of Death Benefit; or
(c) The date of payment of Vesting Benefit; or
(d) The date on expiry of the Revival Period after the Policy has lapsed as per Premium Discontinuance provision; or
(e) The date of payment of free look cancellation amount.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)
payment of Vesting Benefit; or
(d) The date on expiry of the Revival Period after the Policy has lapsed as per Premium Discontinuance provision; or
(e) The date of payment of free look cancellation amount.
Operational Guidelines
35. Is there any discount on this product for ABG employees?
Yes, Guaranteed Addition is enhanced by 2.5% for Limited Pay policies and by 0.5% for Regular Pay policies, if customers buy their policies directly from ABSLI. No commission is payable on these policies.
36. What application form is required for ABSLI Nishchit Pension Plan?
The “Common Application Form MAJOR” would be used for this plan. The form will be duly uploaded in BSMS from where the same can be downloaded.
37. Can Life Insured and Policyholder be different under this plan?
Yes, the Life Insured and Policyholder can be different under this plan. In case Policyholder and the Life Insured are different then all the proceeds for the policy will be paid to the Policyholder.
38. What happens in case of death of the Proposer/ Policyholder?
Where the Life Insured and Proposer/Policyholder are different, on the death of the Proposer/Policyholder, his legal heirs, in accordance with the existing

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)

Where the Life Insured and Proposer/Policyholder are different, on the death of the Proposer/Policyholder, his legal heirs, in accordance with the existing succession laws, will be considered as new Proposer/Policyholder. As there is no death benefit payable on the death of the Proposer/Policyholder, the policy status does not change, and the policy continues.
39. What is the medical grid applicable for this plan? Medical grid of Savings plans is applicable for this plan.
40. Are NRI/ FINO allowed under this plan?
NRI is allowed.
FNIO allowed (however client has to be in India)
41. Is Nishchit Pension Plan available for selling through POS? No, this plan is not available for sale through POS channel.
42. Is the plan allowed under Employer-employee, HUF, Keyman & MWP?
The plan can be issued under Employer-employee ,HUF and MWP. It cannot be issued under Partnership or Keyman.
43. How is this plan different from the ABSLI Empower Pension Plan?
ABSLI Empower Pension is Linked Pension product where the returns are Market linked returns. However, Nishchit Pension Plan is Non – Linked product which offers guaranteed returns to the customer.

The following details are about Nishchit Pension Plan (NPP)
 However, Nishchit Pension Plan is Non – Linked product which offers guaranteed returns to the customer.
44. What will be the target customer segment for this product?
a) The product caters to the need of customers with ‘Low to Moderate’ risk-profile.
